description: Lock glass_lamp_chimneys to early late Roman blown glass oil lamps rather than separate chimney/cove pieces or architectural glass.

checks.reason: The corrected node remains anchored to fourth-century AD Roman glass lamps.
The source says the glass lamps were blown, making blown-glass forming the direct production method.
The scoped object is an operating oil lamp and still requires wick, fuel reservoir, and air-supply architecture.
Architectural flat-glass panes are adjacent material use, not a direct prerequisite for blown glass oil lamps.
The graph should preserve the direct blown_glass production-method edge rather than broad workshop infrastructure.
